Question: A yellow 589-nm light is incident on a surface whose work function is 1.20 eV. What is the stopping potential? What is the cut-off wavelength?

Cut-off frequency for the photoelectric effect in some materials is $$8.0 \times 10^{13} \text{Hz} .$$ When the incident light has a frequency of $$1.2 \times 10^{14} \text{Hz} ,$$ the stopping potential is measured as - 0.16 V. Estimate a value of Planck’s constant from these data (in units $$\text{J} \cdotp \text{s}$$ and $$\text{eV} \cdotp \text{s}$$ ) and determine the percentage error of your estimation.
